name: Pitkern-Norfolk
notes: STUB (deliberate): the grapheme inventory of Pitkern-Norfolk is NOT specified. Pitkern (Pitcairn) / Norfuk (Norfolk Island) is an English-lexifier creole with a Tahitian substrate. English Wikipedia states outright that 'Pitkern spelling is not standardised', and the Norfuk article — while recording that Norfuk was made official by the 2004 Norfolk Island Language Act and that Alice Buffett & Donald Laycock produced a standardised orthography (Speak Norfuk Today, 1988), alongside Beryl Nobbs-Palmer's 1986 dictionary — still notes a lack of standardisation and contains NO letter->IPA table. Buffett's orthography is not available online, and the Omniglot chart is an image. The only IPA on record is A. C. Gimson's transcription of a 1951 Moverley dialogue, which is a text transcription, not a grapheme map. No map is claimed. INPUT CONTRACT (for any future spec): Latin-script Pitkern/Norfuk text, after ONE orthography (realistically Buffett 1988) is obtained and cited.

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors
sources.title: Norfuk language
sources.notes: Standardisation history (Buffett & Laycock 1988; Nobbs-Palmer 1986; 2004 Language Act). Contains no letter->IPA table and still reports a lack of standardisation.

sources.author: Buffett, Alice & Laycock, Donald
sources.title: Speak Norfuk Today: An Encyclopaedia of the Norfolk Island Language
sources.notes: The only credible normative Norfuk orthography. Not available online; not consulted, so no grapheme map is derived from it.

sources.author: Holm, John
sources.title: Pidgins and Creoles, Volume II: Reference Survey
sources.notes: Reference survey of Atlantic and Pacific pidgins/creoles; lexifier and substrate attributions.

sources.author: Michaelis, S.M., Maurer, P., Haspelmath, M. & Huber, M. (eds.)
sources.title: The Atlas of Pidgin and Creole Language Structures (APiCS)
sources.notes: Structural atlas of 76 pidgins/creoles; per-language survey chapters cited in ancestry notes.

ancestors.notes: English (late-18th-century nautical English of the Bounty mutineers) lexifier; Pitkern-Norfolk. Buffett & Laycock (1988); APiCS ch. 24 (Mühlhäusler).
Tahitian substrate (the mutineers' Tahitian partners): vowel system, phonotactics and lexical items. Buffett & Laycock (1988).
